description: Synchronize the canonical project documentation with the owner's confirmed questionnaire answers without changing public pages or application behavior. Establish one authoritative current truth for show duration, troupe size, included experience, setup, venue requirements, birthday personalization, add-ons, geography, owner identity, California start year, and unresolved policy/document questions. Pricing must remain public-facing only as 'From $350'; no audience-size price tiers may be authorized for the website in this task.
non_goals: Do not edit app/, components/, lib/, public/, tests/, runtime configuration, metadata generation, or deployment behavior
Do not publish audience-size price tiers such as 30 / 40 / 50 / 60 children
Do not finalize cancellation, deposit-refund, late-arrival, weather-rescheduling, or penalty policies
Do not claim insurance, background checks, district approval, vendor approval, legal registration, or school documentation without inspected evidence
Do not publish the owner's private residential address
Do not finalize show names, team biographies, awards, social links, logo, character art, or trademark-dependent assets

decision_basis.origin: User-approved seven-task implementation plan after live-site review and owner questionnaire review on 2026-06-27.
investigation_findings: Owner confirmed a typical complete experience of about 60 minutes: about 30 minutes of theatre plus about 30 minutes of games, dancing, bubbles, and interaction
Owner confirmed that a typical production uses three or four performers depending on the show
Owner confirmed scenery, props, sound, games, dancing, and bubbles as part of the standard experience
Owner confirmed setup and pack-down of about 15–20 minutes each and a recommended clear area of about 20 square metres / 215 square feet
Owner confirmed birthday-child participation, name greeting, optional small role, birthday song, finale, small gift, balloons, and family-taken photos
Owner confirmed optional face painting/body art, standalone bubble show, and costumed character appearances
Owner confirmed Svitlana Grygoryshyna as owner and director and California operations since 2022
User explicitly decided that the website must show only 'From $350' until pricing is reconsidered

must_not_violate: docs/core/BRAND.md remains the brand authority: Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre, misslanatheatre.com, info@misslanatheatre.com, (323) 903-2039
Public identity remains professional live children's fairy-tale theatre, not puppet-first entertainment
The site remains English-first and must not use Russian/Slavic brand coding
Owner statements that create legal, safety, privacy, or contractual risk must be recorded as unresolved rather than normalized into public claims
Public pricing canon must be limited to 'From $350' and custom quote language
Historical records and archived reports must not be rewritten as if they were current canon

forbidden_execution_patterns: No blind global replacement of numbers, names, Theater/theatre terms, URLs, or historical text
No conversion of ambiguous owner speech into definite public policy
No invented resolution for unanswered questionnaire fields
No claim that media permissions exist merely because nobody objected
execution_guardrails: Apply semantic edits only to current canonical documents and current-state summaries
Mark each unresolved item as OWNER / LEGAL / DOCUMENT / MEDIA-PERMISSION / POLICY rather than guessing
Preserve American generic SEO keyword variants using 'theater' while keeping the owned brand spelling 'Theatre'
Where duration is normalized, use 'about one hour' and explain the approximate 30 + 30 structure; do not promise exact minute-by-minute timing
Keep pricing language deliberately minimal: 'From $350' plus custom quote factors only
If the task classification appears wrong mid-execution, stop and escalate; do not switch task_mode

readiness_notes: No code changes are authorized in this task

in_scope: Update current canonical facts in docs/core/PROJECT_BRIEF.md, 01_CONTENT_INVENTORY.md, 02_POSITIONING_AND_TONE.md, 03_SITEMAP_AND_SCOPE.md, SITE_STRUCTURE_AND_BLOCKS.md, and related current instructions where the new facts materially apply
Update STATUS.md current snapshot and open-question list without rewriting historical closure records
Create a structured owner-answer decision record separating confirmed public facts, internal operating facts, unsafe statements, and unresolved questions
Replace obsolete current-canon duration statements of 35–50 minutes with the approved approximate one-hour structure
Replace obsolete current-canon audience-size pricing detail with 'From $350' and custom-quote wording
Document the future Planning Your Event information architecture and the rule against repeating technical requirements across marketing pages
out_of_scope: GBP, Search Console, analytics, launch, or noindex removal

steps.title: Inventory current canonical conflicts
actions: Search current canon and STATUS for 35–50 minutes, audience-size pricing tiers, free-within-30-miles claims, two-phone presentation, and old owner/team facts
Classify each occurrence as current canon, historical record, research evidence, or implementation note
Create a conflict matrix in the task report before editing
steps.expectation: Every affected current-canon statement has a documented target state and historical text is protected

steps.title: Create the structured owner-answer decision record
actions: Record confirmed public facts, confirmed internal operations, unresolved policy questions, legal/document verification needs, and media-permission risks
Record the explicit pricing decision: public website uses only 'From $350'; no audience-size tiers
Record the content-distribution rule: light marketing pages, one Planning Your Event page, and booking-specific details after inquiry
steps.expectation: A reviewer can identify exactly what is safe to publish and what remains blocked

steps.title: Synchronize canonical documents
actions: Apply semantic updates to the declared docs/core files
Normalize duration, troupe size, included experience, setup, venue, birthday, add-on, owner, and California-since-2022 facts
Remove current-canon authorization for public audience-size price tiers and unconfirmed travel-zone pricing
Keep cancellation, supervision, physical-contact, vendor, insurance, and media-permission topics explicitly unresolved
steps.expectation: Current canonical documents agree on all newly confirmed facts and no unsafe statement becomes public canon

steps.title: Update current status and backlog
actions: Update STATUS.md top-level current snapshot and open launch dependencies
Preserve historical closure sections unchanged except where an explicit current-status correction is necessary
List the next six implementation TUNG IDs and their dependency order
steps.expectation: STATUS reflects the new truth and points cleanly to implementation work

success_criteria: All current canonical documents consistently use an approximately one-hour complete experience and the approximate 30 + 30 structure
All current canonical public pricing guidance is limited to 'From $350' and custom quote language
No public canon contains audience-size price tiers, a guaranteed free travel radius, or a fixed San Diego surcharge
Confirmed owner facts and unresolved legal/policy/media questions are clearly separated
The planned information architecture keeps technical details off Home and show cards/pages except for minimal essentials and a link to Planning Your Event
No code, runtime, deployment, or frozen governance files changed

required_commands: grep -RInE '35[–-]50|up to ~?15|free within 30 miles|\$400|\$500|\$600' docs/core STATUS.md README.md || true
manual_checks: Review every changed statement against the original owner questionnaire answers
Confirm historical reports and closure records were not rewritten into present-tense canon
Confirm private address, unsafe supervision statements, media assumptions, and unverified vendor/insurance claims are absent from public canon
Confirm the only approved public price expression is 'From $350'
regression_targets: Brand lock and primary contact canon

closure_sequence: Step 1: Scope verification — confirm all steps, success_criteria, and deliverables are complete; confirm execution-safety compliance (no non_goals fixed, no forbidden_execution_patterns violated, execution_guardrails respected)
Step 2: Automated verification — run only the required_commands explicitly listed for the scoped task, confirm all pass
Step 3: Reviewer-grade pre-closure code review — analyze all changes against the review dimensions, record explicit findings with BLOCKER / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NOTE severity
Step 4: Closure resolution — resolve or explicitly defer findings with severity, write the final verification summary, and update the task report
Step 5: PROJECT_PROGRESS update — append the substantive change entry only after all previous closure steps pass

post_actions: Do not begin public-copy implementation until this task's mandatory_closure passes
Use the resulting canon as the authority for the remaining six TUNG tasks
